SUMMARY:
The increase in general public awareness of the links between the Ocean and Climate implies a better understanding of the role of the Ocean in climate

FULL CONTENT:
![Ocean & Climate Platform](https://i0.wp.com/ocean-climate.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/logo-poc.png?fit=400%2C342&ssl=1)

[Ocean & Climate Platform](https://ocean-climate.org/en/home-4/)

A healthy ocean, a protected climate

![Français](data:image/png;base64,)

# Raising awareness

The increase in general public awareness of the links between the Ocean and Climate implies a better understanding of the role of the Ocean in climate regulation, and of the impacts of climate change on the Ocean.

The Ocean and Climate Platform brings together the expertise and competence of its members in order to develop messages along these two themes through education and awareness bringing tools. A working group on knowledge dissemination collaborates with the scientific Committee to provide summaries, briefing notes and short messages for the general public.

--- SOURCE 5: Understanding Ocean Acidification | NOAA Fisheries ---
URL: https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/insight/understanding-ocean-acidification

SUMMARY:
Understanding Ocean Acidification | NOAA Fisheries The Pacific Northwest, Long Island Sound, Narragansett Bay, Chesapeake Bay, Gulf of America (formerly Gulf of Mexico), and areas off Maine and Massachusetts were revealed as hot spots in a 2015 study showing the vulnerability of the $1 billion U.S. shellfish industry to ocean acidification.Also at risk are Alaska’s fisheries, which account for nearly 60 percent of U.S. commercial fish catch and support more than 100,000 jobs. NOAA’s mission is to understand changes in the world’s oceans, share that knowledge, and conserve coastal and marine ecosystems. The program also provides grants for critical research projects that will explore the effects of ocean acidification on marine organisms, ecosystems, and economies.

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://www.ima.gov.tt/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/NOAA-Coral-Reef-on-Ocean-Acidification.jpg
DESCRIPTION: None

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://www.iaea.org/sites/default/files/styles/original_image_size/public/2206worldoceansday2022-whatisoceanacidification-v21cover.png?itok=sSVQfwrh
DESCRIPTION: Fish swim in a deep blue ocean, where carbon dioxide bubbles are depicted rising and dispersing throughout the water, emphasizing the concept of ocean acidification.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://d3583ivmhhw2le.cloudfront.net/images/uploads/blog/Ocean_acidification_101_Credit_SeattleAquarium_preview.jpg
DESCRIPTION: An infographic about ocean acidification with bullet points and a diagram showing how the process works.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://www.herrerainc.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/06/acidification-infographic.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The illustration explains ocean acidification by depicting industrial activities contributing to CO2 emissions, the chemical processes affecting marine life, and highlighting the socioeconomic consequences for coastal regions.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://pwonlyias.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/Untitled-14-1-1.webp
DESCRIPTION: The image illustrates how increased carbon dioxide emissions from power plants lead to ocean acidification, negatively affecting coral reefs and marine life, with visual elements showing factories, a house, a fisherman, and coral that is impacted by the changing ocean chemistry.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

enriched_points.expanded_content: Raising public awareness about ocean acidification is crucial as it empowers communities to understand its impacts and take action in mitigating its effects.
Effective policies are essential to combat ocean acidification; community engagement can drive lawmakers to implement regulations that protect marine ecosystems.
Community involvement fosters local stewardship for oceans, encouraging collaborative efforts in monitoring, research, and conservation practices to address ocean acidification challenges.
